notes_md:
**thêm** = to add, put more in, increase something. It’s used when you want extra quantity or extra items.

You use it with a noun: add food, money, time, details, people, etc. Very common in daily situations like food, drinks, plans, or information.


It can also mean “more” in a general sense, like adding more of something to what’s already there. Think of it as the opposite of **bớt** (reduce, take away).

translation.en: Do you want to add this dish?

content.sentence: Bạn có muốn ___ món này không?

translation.en: I want to add more information.

notes:
thêm = to add, put more in, increase something. It’s used when you want extra quantity or extra items.

You use it with a noun: add food, money, time, details, people, etc. Very common in daily situations like food, drinks, plans, or information.

It can also mean “more” in a general sense, like adding more of something to what’s already there. Think of it as the opposite of bớt (reduce, take away).
exampleSentences: Anh có thể thêm một ít đường vào cà phê cho em không?
Nếu em muốn ăn nhiều hơn, chị sẽ thêm cơm vào bát của em.
Họ quyết định thêm một ngày nghỉ vào chuyến du lịch này.

example.es: Quiero añadir un poco de azúcar al té.
example.en: I want to add a little sugar to the tea.
example.vi: Tôi muốn thêm một ít đường vào trà.

definitions.en: to add; to increase; to supplement

exampleSentencesTranslated: Can you add some sugar to the coffee for me?
If you want to eat more, I will add rice to your bowl.
They decided to add one more day to this trip.
